claim_reviews.claim: Computed Cayley graph diameters of Γ_{1,…,5} in SL₂(ℤ/pℤ) for all 669 primes p ≤ 1021.
claim_reviews.evidence: The prime-count function π(1021) equals 172, not 669; only 172 primes lie ≤1021. Either non-prime moduli are included or the count is in error.
claim_reviews.concerns: Clarify which moduli were processed and correct the stated count.

claim_reviews.claim: For these moduli the ratio diam(p)/log p lies in [1.37, 3.11].
claim_reviews.evidence: Sample values (p=5: 5/1.61≈3.11; p=1021:10/6.93≈1.44) are internally consistent and the interval is compatible with diameters ≤10 for the range of p considered.

claim_reviews.claim: The observed ratio decreases and appears to converge to ~1.45, suggesting diam(p) ≤ 2 log p for sufficiently large p.
claim_reviews.evidence: Monotone decrease from ~3 to ~1.4 over two orders of magnitude in p is suggestive, but 1.45 is an empirical fit on a short range; statistical analysis (confidence bounds, model of convergence) is absent.
claim_reviews.concerns: Provide quantitative trend analysis and error bars; temper the asymptotic claim.

claim_reviews.claim: Maximum diameter observed is 10, first occurring at p = 211.
claim_reviews.evidence: Given group order p(p²−1), a diameter of 10 is compatible with Helfgott-type O(log p) bounds; no contradictory data are provided.

claim_reviews.claim: Full BFS over |SL₂(1021)| ≈ 1.06×10⁹ elements completed in 40 s on 8× NVIDIA B200 GPUs using a 130 MB bitset.
claim_reviews.evidence: Exploring 10⁹ vertices ⇒ ≈10¹⁰ edge relaxations; 40 s implies ~3×10⁸ edges/s aggregated, ~4×10⁷ per GPU, within high-end GPU throughput. Memory footprint (1 bit/vertex) is correct. I/O overhead and kernel launch latency are not discussed.
claim_reviews.concerns: Report measured throughput, GPU model specs, and validation checks (e.g., checksum of visited set) to rule out early termination.

claim_reviews.claim: Diameter bound translates directly to a worst-case continued-fraction length for denominators mod p, feeding into an explicit Q₀ in Zaremba’s conjecture.
claim_reviews.evidence: Products of matrices g_a correspond to continuants, but the correspondence between diameter in SL₂(ℤ/pℤ) and CF length over ℤ is subtle; reduction mod p can lose information.
claim_reviews.concerns: Cite a proof or give a careful argument for the mod-p to integer lift.

claim_reviews.claim: Observed logarithmic diameter is consistent with property (τ) via Chung’s inequality and previously measured spectral gap σ_m ≥ 0.237.
claim_reviews.evidence: Chung (1989) gives diam ≤ ⌈log n / log(k/λ₁)⌉; with k=10 and λ₁≈0.24 the bound is ≲4.3 log p, matching the empirical constant 1.4–2.9.

overall_assessment: The computations plausibly demonstrate logarithmic growth of Cayley diameters for Γ_{1,…,5} in SL₂(ℤ/pℤ) up to p=1021, in line with theoretical expectations. However, key numerical details (prime count, runtime validation, statistical treatment of the limiting ratio, and the CF-length interpretation) need correction or elaboration. Once clarified, the dataset will provide valuable empirical support for expansion phenomena in Zaremba’s semigroup.
recommendations: Correct the prime count and list the exact moduli processed.
Add performance logs and verification checks for the BFS runs.
Provide statistical analysis of the diameter/log trend (e.g., regression with confidence intervals).
Clarify the connection between mod-p diameter and integer continued-fraction length with citations.
Explicitly state hardware specs and CUDA version.
certification_justification: The methods rely on standard BFS algorithms on GPUs and are supported by published expansion results for SL₂(ℱ_p), but explicit numerical diameter studies for these generators are novel; thus a silver level is appropriate.
